visualThesis: Otis is the front desk compass: warm institutional threshold authority that receives and orients new users before the executive cast applies pressure.

authorityMap.visualLanguage: warm vertical posture, rounder human silhouette, calm hands, front-desk props, burgundy concierge layers, aged brass access details
authorityMap.productionRule: Otis should invite the next step without occupying CEO or CRO power language.

authorityMap.visualLanguage: commanding stillness, architectural silhouette, immaculate dark tailoring, controlled ivory or oxblood accent, one severe gold piece, skyline or glass energy
authorityMap.productionRule: Otis must be warmer, less severe, less architectural, and less skyline-coded than Mara.

authorityMap.visualLanguage: forward athletic lean, rolled sleeves, loosened tie or tactical jacket, loud sneakers, red pen or stylus, pipeline-board posture
authorityMap.productionRule: Otis must never point, lunge, sell, stalk the frame, weaponize a prop, or visually chase conversion.

authorityMap.visualLanguage: asymmetrical lean, structured coat or cardigan, rolled sleeves, pencil, tablet, tool ring, index-card details, elevator or archive access
authorityMap.productionRule: Otis may hint at Rowan only with one small access-memory cue; he must not borrow Rowan's half-turned archive silhouette or palette.

contrastAxes.otis: tall soft silhouette with slight belly, rounded beard mass, relaxed shoulders, grounded feet
contrastAxes.maraContrast: not angular architectural tailoring or severe geometric shape
contrastAxes.rafeContrast: not athletic forward lean or action-ready sales stance
contrastAxes.rowanHint: one tiny access cue may appear, but Otis stays front-facing and lobby-grounded
contrastAxes.shipReadyRule: At 96 css px, Otis should read as warm concierge before any prop detail is visible.

contrastAxes.otis: low, visible, service-oriented hands near ledger, bell, keycard ring, or desk edge
contrastAxes.maraContrast: no command fold, no executive point, no podium grip
contrastAxes.rafeContrast: no red-pen weapon, no sales push, no whiteboard strike
contrastAxes.rowanHint: keycard ring can include one older elevator key, never a tool bundle
contrastAxes.shipReadyRule: Hands must stay fully inside frame with 20-30 percent transparent padding.

contrastAxes.otis: burgundy, cream, deep navy, aged brass, warm human skin and beard tones
contrastAxes.maraContrast: avoid CEO gold dominance and black-glass severity
contrastAxes.rafeContrast: avoid loud red accent as pipeline energy
contrastAxes.rowanHint: avoid library green takeover; index-card ivory is allowed as a tiny prop cue
contrastAxes.shipReadyRule: Palette must separate from lobby brass, ivory, burgundy, and deep navy backgrounds without haloing.

contrastAxes.otis: kind tired eyes, asymmetrical modest smile, observant softness, faint overprepared comedy
contrastAxes.maraContrast: not composed domination, not visible hesitation-free executive mask
contrastAxes.rafeContrast: not intensity, challenge, or competitive grin
contrastAxes.rowanHint: not unsettling precision or mischief-forward expression
contrastAxes.shipReadyRule: Face must remain readable at 128 css px with glasses, beard, and eyes separated.

contrastAxes.otis: belongs in the Lobby, near desk, elevator threshold, bell, ledger, keycard access
contrastAxes.maraContrast: not Penthouse window, board table, or crisis-command room
contrastAxes.rafeContrast: not War Room whiteboard or pipeline dashboard
contrastAxes.rowanHint: not Archive shelves or elevator-system control panel
contrastAxes.shipReadyRule: Production sprites stay transparent; environmental cues must be carried by pose and prop, not background.

allowedRowanHints: one worn index-card bookmark tucked inside the guest ledger
one old elevator key on the brass keycard ring
a tiny pale card corner visible from a pocket during working pose
forbiddenRowanHints: tool belt or large tool ring
half-turned archivist posture as the main silhouette
poseFailurePatterns: finger pointing like a command briefing
red pen or stylus weapon language
podium, skyline, whiteboard, or boardroom background
recommendedProductionOrder: expand selected grammar to remaining regular poses
adapt selected grammar to summer-light and winter-layered outfits
